question: How should support handle the issue described as 5697wsz01 DSPDEST zcentric routopts workstation?
reference_answer: The communication between the zCentric agents and the CONTROLLER is quite a bit different from anything TWSz has used before. There are no persistent connections, but rather two independent, two-way connections. The Agent's HTTP SERVER waits for the CONTROLLER's HTTP CLIENT to send it orders (such as: run a job, send me a joblog, please report status, etc.), and responds to those requests. The CONTROLLER's HTTP SERVER waits for the Agent's HTTP CLIENT to send ending status for scheduled jobs. After each exchange, the connection is held open for five minutes to reduce reconnection overhead in case there is something more to say. If nothing transpires in these five minutes, then it is closed. When the CONTROLLER starts up, it does not attempt to connect to a zCentric agent until the first job is submitted on that workstation. The workstation is normally set to ACTIVE with an extended status of "waiting for first submission". When the first job is submitted, the connection is opened and the workstation is set to ACTIVE. If the workstation is in OFFLINE or UNKNOWN status and you attempt to manually set it to ACTIVE, a message (EQQWL17W) is issued: > WORKSTATION CANNOT BE VARIED TO ACTI

question: How should support handle the issue described as group filters; user filters; filter; map?
reference_answer: This behaviour is by design. Only global and system filters are available for selection for use in maps. The reasoning behind this is that when access to a map page or portlet is granted to a user or group, it is not possible at that point to determine whether this user or group has permissions to view all of the filters within the map or only some of them. This access control is therefore enforced at the time of map creation, by restricting the filters available for selection.

reference_answer: AIX Mustgather full core files settings setup crash diagnostic; MustGatherDocument TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING) PROBLEM(ABSTRACT) When a crash occurs, the most important diagnostic data to obtain is the process core file. In order to ensure this is generated for the Java Virtual Machine (JVM) on AIX, there are a number of settings that need to be in place. The majority of the JVM settings are in place by default, but operating system settings also need to be in place. RESOLVING THE PROBLEM Operating system settings: 1. To obtain full core files set the following ulimit options: ulimit -c unlimited - turn on corefiles with unlimited size ulimit -d unlimited - sets the user data limit to unlimited ulimit -f unlimited - sets the file limit to unlimited The current ulimit settings can be displayed using: ulimit -a These values are the "soft" limit, and are done on a per user basis. These cannot exceed the "hard" limit value. In order to display and change to the "hard" limits, the same ulimit commands can be run using the additional -H flag. question: What fix, workaround, or guidance is documented for ISD WISD log director?
reference_answer: ISD WISD log director TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING) PROBLEM(ABSTRACT) Always ON WISD jobs don't show any changes in the Director log when an operation is invoked. RESOLVING THE PROBLEM This affects WISD/ISD jobs that have both a WISD input and output and are always ON and multiple instance jobs. This means that each request sent to the service will be processed in one of the instances with a unique instance id. The job continues to run after this request is handled and the log file may not show any changes. In order to better view the WISD log files follow these steps: * Always view the main job and not the specific instance. For example you would want to click the log view for "WISDtest" and not one with an invocation id at the end. The main job will be in a compiled state: WISDtest WISDtest.1287592458075 WISDtest.1287592458076 WISDtest.1287592458077 This job log will show all the logs for each instance. * When in the log view click the column "Invocation ID" to sort the instances to easily see all entries for that instance. * Create a peek stage within the job. question: What fix, workaround, or guidance is documented for DOORS; Server; Certificate; Security?
reference_answer: Simplest way to enable 'secure' connection Using Default Certificate: 1. use default certificates 2. add a rule to 'hosts' file to map 'IBEDSERV' to the server's ip (if client will be run in a different machine, 'hosts' file of both client and server should be updated for this). 3. run doorsd with '-secure ON' flag (make sure server side security is disabled with '-serverSecurityDisable': doorsd.exe -debug -s "C:\data\official_9.5" -p 36700 -serverhostname IBMEDSERV -secure ON -serverSecurityDisable 4. run DOORS client: doors.exe -data 36700@IBMEDSERV Client should run without any problems. Once this is tested and verified, repeat the test above with custom certificates. Note: If for example, certificates are generated with a different name than IBMEDSERV, server and client should be started with that name. Once the 'secure' mode are configured and tested. Then test server side security. Enable server side security: 1. Start a broker from DWA installation 2. Restart 'doorsd' by enabling server side security: doorsd.exe -debug -s "C:\data\official_9.5" -p 36700 -serverhostname IBMEDSERV -secure ON -serverSecurityEnable 3. Run an interop server: doors.exe -interop -data 36700@IBMEDSE

question: What does the TechQA document say about new feature; spam prevention; spam; Block mail from sender; messages; email; e-mail; add; adding; added; sender; Block Mail list; block mail; block; blocking; blocked; Rules folder?
reference_answer: This issue was reported to Quality Engineering as SPR# LROE5MJHCV. The limit is not defined by the number of Internet addresses/Notes user names but currently by the size of the Block Mail From Sender list. The list is limited to 32K worth of data. One character is equivalent to one byte, except when using double-byte character languages, where one character takes two bytes. An Enhancement Request has been submitted to Quality Engineering in SPR MCMA6XLKLE, to request for a new feature which will notify users when the limit has been reached. In addition, an Enhancement Request to increase the limit has been submitted to Quality Engineering as well in SPR ARUI6TZR5P. NOTE: In mail templates prior to 6.0.5/6.5.4, the limit was 64K of data but this resulted in undesired behavior and the design was revised to 32K. If you are running a Notes/Domino client release or template version prior to 6.0.5/6.5.4, refer to Document #1162013 [http://www.ibm.com/support/docview.wss?rs=474&uid=swg21162013], "Domino 6.5.x Router performance, hang or crashes delivering messages to mail file using Block Sender rule," for specific changes you can make to mail templates. question: What fix, workaround, or guidance is documented for redhat rhel soft lockup defect performance cpu problem db2 bigsql?
reference_answer: redhat rhel soft lockup defect performance cpu problem db2 bigsql TECHNOTE (TROUBLESHOOTING) PROBLEM(ABSTRACT) The RHEL Soft Lockup defect can cause huge CPU performance problems for DB2 and BigSQL. SYMPTOM When the database seems to slow down, a quick running utility doesn't finish, or a shutdown fails for no obvious reason then go look in the OS logs for messages about "soft lockup." This event is known to cause serious issues for DB2 databases running on RHEL Linux. The /var/log/messages file will have entries like these: kernel:BUG: soft lockup - CPU#4 stuck for 67s! [db2fmp:8217] kernel:BUG: soft lockup - CPU#0 stuck for 67s! [db2bp:27662] kernal:BUG: soft lockup - CPU#3 stuck for 67s! (db2sysc:24039) kernal:BUG: soft lockup - CPU#5 stuck for 67s! (db2sysc:24126) kernal:BUG: soft lockup - CPU#3 stuck for 67s! (db2sysc:24039) DIAGNOSING THE PROBLEM When these events are occurring the vmstat will usually show a backup in the run queue column. This is indicative of threads that are ready to run but cannot get any CPU time. On a system behaving normally vmstat shows no build up in the run queue: procs -----------memory---------- ---swap-- -----io---- --system-- -----cpu----- r b s
